behaviors.description: `=SUM(A1:A10)` returns the correct sum for values in `A1..A10`.
behaviors.justification: Real failures are often in parser/range-resolution/function-registry wiring, not SUM arithmetic alone. A unit test of SUM could pass while cell-range binding fails.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real workbook in memory with `A1..A10` populated, set `B1` to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, run the real evaluation pipeline (parser + reference resolver + function dispatcher), and assert `B1` equals expected total.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a formula cell (e.g., `B1=A1*2`, `C1=B1+1`) produces correct cascaded output.
behaviors.justification: Needs dependency resolution plus expression evaluation across cells. Mocking either side can hide propagation bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Using the real workbook engine, set `A1=5`, `B1==A1*2`, `C1==B1+1`, evaluate all, assert `B1=10` and `C1=11`; then change `A1` and re-evaluate to confirm cascade remains correct.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (`A1 -> B1 -> A1`) returns a circular reference error, not a loop.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection must be enforced in full evaluation flow; isolated graph-unit tests can miss engine-level loop-prevention or error surfacing issues.
behaviors.test_description: Set `A1==B1+1` and `B1==A1+1` in a real workbook instance, run evaluation with real dependency traversal, assert explicit circular-reference error type/code and bounded runtime (no hang).

behaviors.description: `VLOOKUP` exact-match returns correct value and missing-key returns error.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on interaction between function semantics and table/range addressing; mocking lookup table access can hide mis-indexing bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Build a sheet with a lookup table range, evaluate real `VLOOKUP(...,FALSE)` formulas for one existing key and one missing key, assert exact returned value and expected not-found error.

behaviors.description: Nested `IF` conditions evaluate to correct branch (`high`/`medium`/`low`).
behaviors.justification: Nested expression parsing + conditional evaluation across cell references is the failure boundary; pure branch-function unit tests may miss parse/eval integration defects.
behaviors.test_description: With real parser/evaluator, set `A1` to representative values (e.g., 5, 15, 25), evaluate `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`, assert expected output for each value.

behaviors.description: `CONCATENATE` joins values with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: Skipping empties depends on how cell emptiness is represented and passed through evaluator. A mocked function input can miss real empty-cell semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Populate cells with text/empty values, evaluate real formula like `=CONCATENATE(A1,"-",A2,"-",A3)` (or equivalent), assert output omits empty-cell contribution per product rule.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (`=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves when target sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Requires workbook multi-sheet namespace resolution plus evaluator wiring; unit tests of arithmetic/reference tokenization alone can miss sheet-scoping bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with `Sheet1` and `Sheet2`, set `Sheet2!A1`, set `Sheet1!B1==Sheet2!A1+1`, evaluate, and assert correct value in `Sheet1!B1` with real reference resolver.

behaviors.description: When an input cell changes, dependents recalculate in correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: This is dependency-graph scheduling behavior across modules; mocking scheduler or evaluator can hide stale-value/order bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Set a dependency chain/diamond (e.g., `B1=A1+1`, `C1=B1*2`, `D1=B1+C1`), evaluate, mutate `A1`, trigger recalc, and assert final values reflect correct ordered propagation (no stale intermediates).

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores formula text and prior dependent computed values.
behaviors.justification: Needs real interaction between edit history/state snapshots and recalculation engine. Unit-testing undo stack alone can miss dependent-value restoration bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create dependent formulas, evaluate baseline, edit an upstream formula and re-evaluate, invoke real undo API, then assert restored formula text and all downstream values match pre-edit baseline.

behaviors.description: Save/load workbook in custom binary format preserves formulas, values, and floating-point precision.
behaviors.justification: Real serialization/deserialization with actual filesystem I/O is required; in-memory mocks can hide binary encoding, field-order, and precision round-trip failures.
behaviors.test_description: Using a temp file, create workbook with representative formulas and sensitive float values, save via real binary writer, reload via real reader, and assert formula strings and numeric values are equal within strict tolerance/no unexpected precision drift.
